pages.title: Wooden stave
pages.text: The basic stave is a short diagonal recipe: upper-right plank, lower-left stick.$(br2)It is not the vertical tool-handle recipe used by vanilla tools.

pages.title: Inventory 2x2
pages.text: Your inventory grid works:$(br2)$(l)Empty  Plank$()$(br)$(l)Stick   Empty$()$(br2)If you place it vertically or mirror the positions, no result appears.

pages.title: Upgrade recipe view
pages.text: Upgrades use the same short-stave shape: next material in the upper-right, old stave in the lower-left.$(br2)The custom recipe checks the old stave tier and required next material.

pages.title: Upgrade recipe
pages.text: Upgrades use the same diagonal shape:$(br2)$(l)Empty  Next material$()$(br)$(l)Old stave Empty$()$(br2)Material order: stone material → copper ingot → iron ingot → gold ingot → diamond → netherite ingot.

pages.title: Momentum and 劫
pages.text: Successfully casting an implemented glyph grants 1 brush momentum to the held stave. Clearing $(l)入$() grants extra momentum: wood +5, stone +8, copper +10, iron/gold +12, diamond/netherite +15.$(br2)When the stave is full, write $(l)劫$() and clear the tribulation arena to mark it upgrade-ready.

pages.title: Same-tier repair
pages.text: On an anvil, put the stave you want to keep in slot one and another same-tier stave in slot two.$(br2)The output keeps slot one momentum, tribulation state, name, and extra data. Slot two is only consumed as repair material.

pages.title: Ink core repair
pages.text: You can also place $(l)ink cores$() in the second anvil slot. Each core repairs a fixed 250 durability, up to 4 cores per operation.$(br2)Ink cores repair durability only. They do not grant momentum or transfer tribulation state.

pages.title: Tier meaning
pages.text: Wood: entry. Stone: stable. Copper: utility. Iron: standard combat. Gold: strong but short-lived. Diamond: advanced mechanics. Netherite: endgame stability.$(br2)Higher staves usually increase range, duration, cap, or add a secondary mechanic.
